Buildertrend vs Autodesk Build (2026)

Residential powerhouse vs commercial enterprise platform. These two rarely compete for the same customer — but here's how they stack up.

✓ Updated April 2026

Buildertrend and Autodesk Build are both construction management platforms, but they serve distinctly different markets. Buildertrend was built for residential builders and remodelers. Autodesk Build was built for commercial GCs, design-build firms, and enterprise construction organizations. Understanding this distinction makes the comparison straightforward for most contractors.

Short answer: If you build or remodel homes, Buildertrend is the right choice. If you run commercial projects with multiple primes, BIM coordination, and complex document management needs, Autodesk Build is the right choice. The overlap zone — mid-size GCs doing mixed residential and commercial — is where the real comparison happens.

Side-by-side comparison

Feature Buildertrend Autodesk Build
Starting price $499/mo (all users) Custom — requires sales call
Target market Residential builders, remodelers Commercial GCs, enterprise construction
Homeowner/client portal ✓ Excellent — central feature Limited — not a focus
BIM integration Limited ✓ Deep — built on Autodesk ecosystem
Document management Good for residential ✓ Enterprise-grade, version control
Selections management ✓ Built for homebuilders Not included
RFI management Basic ✓ Full workflow with audit trail
Submittal tracking Limited ✓ Complete submittal log
Estimating ✓ Built in Module (TakeoffManager)
BuildingConnected integration No ✓ Native (both Autodesk)
Mobile app quality ✓ Strong for field use ✓ Strong for commercial field
Accounting integration QuickBooks, Xero Sage, Oracle, SAP, custom ERP

Pricing comparison

Buildertrend

Buildertrend uses flat monthly pricing: $499/month includes all users — office staff, field crews, and subcontractors. No per-seat fees, no overage charges. Predictable, and very favorable for teams with large field headcounts. The flat pricing makes it easier to budget and easier to justify adding more users.

Autodesk Build

Autodesk Build pricing is custom and based on the number of users, modules purchased, and project volume. It's part of the Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) suite, which can include Autodesk Build, BuildingConnected, Takeoff, and other modules. Expect pricing in the range of $1,000–$5,000+/month for most commercial GC deployments. Large enterprise firms may spend significantly more. The value-add for commercial firms is substantial, but so is the investment.

Where Buildertrend wins

Homeowner experience

The Buildertrend client portal is genuinely excellent. Homeowners see their project timeline, approve selections (cabinet finishes, flooring, fixtures), view daily logs and photos, sign change orders, and communicate with the builder — all from an app on their phone. For custom home builders and remodelers where client communication is a competitive differentiator, this feature alone justifies the platform. Autodesk Build simply doesn't have an equivalent — it's built for owner organizations, not individual homeowners.

Residential workflow depth

Buildertrend understands residential: selections management, warranty requests, lead management, and the financial flow of residential construction (customer deposits, draw schedules, final payments) are all built in. Autodesk Build's workflow assumes commercial project structure — design drawings, submittals, RFIs, multi-party contracts — which doesn't map to a custom home.

Cost and simplicity

At $499/month flat, Buildertrend is accessible for contractors with 5–50 employees. Autodesk Build at commercial pricing would be 3–10x the cost for a similar team. For residential contractors, that premium buys nothing useful.

Try Buildertrend →

Where Autodesk Build wins

BIM coordination and design integration

Autodesk Build is part of the Autodesk ecosystem that includes Revit, AutoCAD, Navisworks, and ACC. BIM models created in Revit connect directly to Autodesk Build for clash detection, model-based RFIs, and design coordination. For commercial projects where BIM is required or valuable, this integration is a significant advantage. No other platform matches Autodesk's BIM-to-build continuity.

Document management at scale

Commercial projects generate thousands of documents: drawings with multiple revisions, submittals, RFIs, change orders, inspection reports, O&M manuals. Autodesk Build's document management handles version control, access permissions, approval workflows, and audit trails at a scale that residential tools aren't designed for. When you need to prove that the sub used Drawing Rev 3, not Rev 2, Autodesk Build has the record.

BuildingConnected integration

The native integration between Autodesk Build and BuildingConnected (both Autodesk products) creates a preconstruction-to-execution workflow where bid data flows directly into the project without re-entry. Subcontractor information from BuildingConnected becomes the project directory in Autodesk Build. For commercial GCs who use BuildingConnected for bid solicitation, this alone saves significant time.

Enterprise integrations

Large commercial firms run SAP, Oracle, Sage 300 CRE, or CMiC for their financial systems. Autodesk Build integrates with all of these. Buildertrend integrates with QuickBooks and Xero — appropriate for smaller businesses but not enterprise ERP systems.

Who should choose Buildertrend

  • Custom home builders and high-end remodelers where client experience matters
  • Residential GCs with 2–50 employees who want one platform for everything
  • Contractors doing primarily residential work who occasionally do light commercial
  • Anyone who wants flat, predictable pricing without enterprise contract negotiations

Who should choose Autodesk Build

  • Commercial GCs doing $20M+ in annual revenue with complex project requirements
  • Firms doing design-build where BIM coordination is part of the workflow
  • Companies that already use Autodesk's design tools (Revit, AutoCAD) and want continuity
  • Organizations that use BuildingConnected for bid solicitation and want a single-vendor ecosystem

The hybrid contractor question

What about GCs who do both residential custom homes and commercial projects? Most end up choosing based on their revenue mix and using the platform for both types of work, accepting that it fits one side better. A contractor doing 70% residential and 30% commercial is almost always better served by Buildertrend; a contractor doing the reverse should look at Autodesk Build or Procore.

Bottom line

This isn't really a close comparison for most contractors. Buildertrend is the right choice for residential. Autodesk Build is the right choice for commercial enterprise. The key is being honest about which category you actually fall into — and not over-buying Autodesk Build because it seems more sophisticated, or under-investing in Buildertrend because the commercial features seem more impressive.

Buildertrend — the complete platform for residential builders. Flat pricing, homeowner portal, selections, and full project management.

Start with Buildertrend →
